instruction: In a study to estimate the proportion of residents in a city that support the construction of a new bypass road in the vicinity, a random sample of $2025$ residents were polled. Let $X$ denote the number in the sample who supported the proposal. To estimate the true proportion in support of the plan, we can compute $\hat{p}=\frac{X+\sqrt{2025}/2}{2025}.$ The estimator $\hat{p}$ has bias [ANS] A. $1/(2 \times 45)$.  B. 0, so unbiased.  C. $1/45$.  D. $1/2025$.  E. $45/2$.
